response.justification: Bogdan has genuine geospatial domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map, and meaningful distributed-systems exposure from Sociomantic's high-throughput pipeline work, but the role is fundamentally a C++/Rust systems engineering position and his background is overwhelmingly TypeScript/Node.js. His only commercial C++ experience is a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015, and there is no Rust exposure at all. Without demonstrated modern C++ at production scale — the explicit primary requirement — the domain fit alone is insufficient to advance.
strengths: Direct geospatial domain experience: founded and operates GISCollective, a production geo-data platform, and built GIS tooling for Green Map — genuine familiarity with the problem space

Distributed-systems exposure at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O, caching, and high-performance data stores — architectural thinking relevant to a routing pipeline
Broad polyglot systems background (DLang, C/C++, Linux) and a pattern of tackling outside-expertise problems, which aligns with the role's stated expectation to expand competencies
concerns: No modern C++ at production scale: the only commercial C++ work is a six-month role in 2014–2015; the primary language requirement is essentially unmet
No Rust experience whatsoever, eliminating the alternative path the JD explicitly offers
No routing, navigation-guidance, or tile-generation experience — the core pipeline domain — despite adjacent GIS work
